mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and signals easier financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the recent trend, real rates and expected policy restraint rise, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and slower future earnings growth, so a persistent increase in unemployment should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not negative in a majority of periods after a 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the sign relationship flips for more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS slowdown together with a rising UNRATE marks demand deterioration that should weaken sales expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change falls below zero while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-payroll/unemployment trigger does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of events, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS reflects stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly RSAFS growth is not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST after a prior slump signals a housing-cycle rebound that improves cyclical growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 3 months after at least 2 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound triggers do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect disappears once GDPC1 is removed from the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Higher INDPRO indicates stronger industrial production and better real activity, which supports earnings revisions and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when production momentum is improving.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-to-monthly rises in INDPRO are not associated with positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign effect is unstable across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO weakens while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, the combination signals restrictive policy against a soft real economy, which should create negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth turns negative while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint restrictive-policy/weak-output condition fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the effect is present when only one proxy is active.
